label: Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs

node.label: Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs
node.definition: Cryptographic signatures and append-only logs authenticate artifacts, requests, model versions, and lineage events while making unauthorized modification detectable.
node.why_it_matters: They provide an integrity substrate for provenance, audit, and controlled agent execution.
limitations: Keys, identities, and trust roots must be managed securely, and signed data can still be false at creation time.

dependencies.rationale: Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs turns Distributed systems & networking into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs turns Content credentials, signing & provenance into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs turns Algorithms & data structures into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
